paper.title: SGDR: Stochastic Gradient Descent with Warm Restarts

claims.text: SGDR with warm restarts requires 2-4× fewer epochs than standard learning rate schedules to achieve comparable or better results
SGDR achieves state-of-the-art results of 3.14% test error on CIFAR-10 and 16.21% on CIFAR-100 when combined with snapshot ensembles
Cosine annealing learning rate schedule with periodic restarts improves anytime performance compared to step decay schedules
Snapshot ensembles from SGDR restarts provide 3-fold speedup compared to training independent models for ensemble construction

methods.name: SGDR
methods.description: Stochastic Gradient Descent with warm restarts using cosine annealing learning rate schedule. Learning rate is periodically reset to maximum value and annealed down following a cosine curve.
methods.runnable_hint: Implement SGD with momentum on a toy 2D quadratic: x,y = np.random.randn(2)*10; v = np.zeros(2); for epoch in range(200): for t in range(10): T_cur = (epoch*10+t) % T_i; eta = eta_min + 0.5*(eta_max-eta_min)*(1+np.cos(np.pi*T_cur/T_i)); grad = 2*np.array([x,y]); v = 0.9*v - eta*grad; x,y = x+v[0], y+v[1]; if (epoch+1)%T_i==0: print(f'Restart at epoch {epoch+1}, loss={x**2+y**2:.4f}'). Set T_i=50, eta_max=0.1, eta_min=0.0.

params.name: T_mult
params.description: Factor to increase restart period after each restart
